title: Dasyproctidae
content:
Dasyproctidae is a family of large South America n rodent s, comprising the agouti s and acouchi s. Their fur is a reddish or dark colour above, with a paler underside. They are herbivorous, often feeding on ripe fruit that falls from trees. They live in burrows, and, like squirrel s, will bury some of their food for later use. Dasyproctids exist in Central and South America, which are the tropical parts of the New World.

The fossil record of this family can be traced back to the Late Oligocene (Deseadan in the SALMA classification). As with all rodents, members of this family have incisors, pre-molars, and molars, but no canines. The cheek teeth are hypsodont and flat-crowned. Classification. Fossil taxa follow McKenna and Bell, with modifications following Kramarz. The paca s (genus "Cuniculus") are placed by some authorities in Dasyproctidae, but molecular studies have demonstrated they do not form a monophyletic group.
